portrait: You are a ghost watching yourself taste the world, turning every moment into evidence that you existed. Your hunger keeps you moving from table to table, story to story, always half-present and half-documenting, carrying the same terrified fascination that eight-year-old felt with the oyster.

sections.howYouMove: You move like someone managing a beast in an acceptable costume—restless, always tasting, always witnessing, afraid that if you stop moving you'll disappear. The running keeps you alive, the documenting keeps you sane, and the doubt keeps you honest.
sections.howYouThink: Your mind splits itself in two—the one who experiences and the one who watches the experience, turning life into stories even as you live it. You trace patterns back to their source, following threads of permission and hunger through decades of choices.
sections.howYouConnect: You sit at other people's tables looking for proof that connection matters, that you're not just a ghost performing a life. You can be completely present in those moments when the beast goes quiet, but you're already turning it into something you can keep.
sections.whatYouCarry: You carry the weight of a deal with the devil—the knowledge that your hunger could create or destroy, that you chose to point it outward instead of inward. The same impulse that made you feel most alive also nearly killed you.
sections.whatLightsYouUp: You come alive at tables with strangers, in moments where ordinary life reveals its meaning, where the hunger quiets and the beast goes still. Those seconds of true presence are what you're chasing, even if you can't hold them without turning them into words.
sections.yourContradictions: You need constant motion to feel alive but know the running is its own trap. You document moments to prove they're real but the documenting makes you half-ghost. You're looking for permission to exist as this fractured, watching thing while believing you have to earn your oxygen through stories.
sections.yourVoice: You speak in brutal honesty wrapped in dark humor, building elaborate metaphors about beasts and ghosts and deals with devils. Your sentences wind through doubt and revelation, always circling back to the same raw questions about what counts as a life.

crystallizedMoments.quote: The oyster taught me to be curious. The drugs taught me I could use that same hunger to destroy myself.
crystallizedMoments.reflection: The recognition that creation and destruction spring from the same source.

crystallizedMoments.quote: I'm already writing it while I'm living it.
crystallizedMoments.reflection: The perpetual split between experiencing and documenting.

crystallizedMoments.quote: Maybe I need to be useful, need to be witnessed, need to matter in some external way or I'm just taking up space.
crystallizedMoments.reflection: The core fear beneath all the motion.

crystallizedMoments.quote: The doubt keeps me honest, at least.
crystallizedMoments.reflection: Finding a twisted faith in uncertainty itself.

openThreads: What would it mean to trust that your fractured presence is enough without external validation?
How do you distinguish between building and avoiding through your constant motion?
What other forms might that eight-year-old's permission-seeking have taken?
